title: Chamaemelum grisleyi
intro: Chamaemelum nobile, also known as Chamomile, is a small daisy-like perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family. The health benefits of Chamaemelum nobile include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia due to its calming effects. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as apigenin, and sesquiterpene lactones, such as chamazulene, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Chamaemelum nobile is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, as well as being incorporated into herbal supplements and essential oils for its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Chamaemelum grisleyi belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It is a member of the genus Chamaemelum, which comprises several species of low-growing, spreading plants. The genus Chamaemelum is further classified under the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that includes many species of asters and daisies. Within the order Asterales, the family Asteraceae is the largest and most diverse group, comprising over 23,000 species. Chamaemelum grisleyi is also classified under the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plants that are capable of photosynthesis.
